title: Build Memory Puzzle Game with Multiple Game Types

requirements.text: Create a card matching memory game where players flip cards to find matching pairs, with a grid that starts at 4x4 and expands as difficulty increases

quotes: Multiple Puzzle Types: Jigsaw puzzles, sliding puzzles, pattern matching, aur sequence memory

acceptanceCriteria: Card grid displays face-down cards that flip on click
Matched pairs remain face-up
Unmatched pairs flip back after brief delay
Grid size increases with difficulty level

requirements.text: Implement a sliding puzzle game where players rearrange numbered tiles by sliding them into an empty space to restore correct order
acceptanceCriteria: Grid displays numbered tiles with one empty space
Tiles adjacent to empty space can slide into it
Puzzle is solvable from shuffled starting state
Victory condition detects correct tile order

requirements.text: Build a pattern memory game where players must replicate increasingly complex visual patterns shown briefly then hidden
acceptanceCriteria: Pattern displays briefly then hides
Player attempts to recreate pattern
System validates player input against original pattern
Pattern complexity increases with level

requirements.text: Create a sequence memory game where players must remember and repeat sequences of items in correct order
acceptanceCriteria: Sequence displays one item at a time
Player must repeat sequence in correct order
Incorrect order results in failure
Sequence length increases with difficulty

requirements.text: Implement automatic progressive difficulty system that increases challenge after each completed level across all puzzle types

quotes: Auto Progressive Difficulty: Game automatically harder hota jayega jaise player progress karega

acceptanceCriteria: Difficulty level increases after successful puzzle completion
Each puzzle type adjusts appropriately (more cards, larger grids, longer sequences)
Difficulty progression is consistent across game types
System tracks current difficulty level

requirements.text: Add score tracking system with timer that records completion time and calculates scores based on speed and accuracy

quotes: Score tracking, timer, aur level progress

acceptanceCriteria: Timer starts when puzzle begins
Score calculated based on completion time and moves/attempts
Scores display after puzzle completion
High scores persist across sessions

requirements.text: Create level progress tracking system that shows player advancement through difficulty levels and unlocked puzzle types
acceptanceCriteria: Current level displays for each puzzle type
Progress indicator shows advancement
Level data persists in backend
Player can view overall progress across all puzzle types

requirements.text: Integrate Internet Identity authentication for secure user login and personalized game data
acceptanceCriteria: Login button triggers Internet Identity flow
Authenticated users can save progress
User identity persists across sessions
Logout functionality available

requirements.text: Build Motoko backend to store game state, high scores, and player progress with queries to retrieve player-specific data

quotes: Backend Storage: Motoko mein game state aur high scores save honge
Backend storage for game state and high scores using Motoko

acceptanceCriteria: Backend stores individual player game states
High scores saved per player and puzzle type
Level progress persists across sessions
Query functions retrieve player-specific data

requirements.text: Design a modern, responsive game interface using React and Tailwind CSS with clean visual hierarchy and engaging brain training theme

quotes: Beautiful Interface: React + Tailwind CSS se modern UI

acceptanceCriteria: Interface is responsive across device sizes
Visual theme conveys brain training and cognitive improvement
Clear navigation between puzzle types
Game controls are intuitive and accessible

constraints: Must use single-actor Motoko backend architecture
Frontend must use React with TypeScript and Tailwind CSS
Authentication must use Internet Identity only
Do not modify immutable frontend paths (useInternetIdentity.ts, useActor.ts, main.tsx, components/ui)
nonGoals: External AI or machine learning analysis
Third-party authentication providers beyond Internet Identity

required: Brain icon with puzzle pieces for app logo (brain-logo.dim_128x128.png)
Card back design with neural network pattern for memory matching game (card-back.dim_200x200.png)
Abstract geometric pattern background for pattern memory game (pattern-bg.dim_800x600.png)
Trophy icon for high score display (trophy.dim_64x64.png)
